topic: In China employee termination: FAQ-style legal and payroll explainer for foreign clients
primary_keyword: China employee termination FAQ for foreign employers
related_keywords: China severance guide for foreign companies

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Primary legal anchor for termination and compensation exposure; essential for foreign-reader legal context.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Explains offboarding-linked statutory obligations relevant to payroll closure.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
sources.fit_note: Adds accessible payroll-tax context for non-local operators.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
sources.fit_note: Bridges legal framework and multinational execution reality; suitable for foreign-reader interpretation risk context.

consensus: Termination in China should be managed as a legal-regulatory process with payroll consequences.
Documentation quality and communication consistency materially affect dispute risk.
When uncertainty exists, employers should separate uncontested from contested items before payout.
uncertainties: City-level evidence expectations and dispute handling can vary by case facts.
Certain component-level treatment details may require local confirmation.
English-language summaries may not reflect all Chinese-language implementation detail.
source_fit_assessment: Sources are sufficient for FAQ-first audience fit: two official legal anchors, one practical tax reference, and one multinational practitioner analysis. No source replacement required this run.
